input_summary: A maintainer has built `cicd-audit`, a zero-dependency CI/CD security auditor with strong README positioning, packaging metadata, tests, and multiple report formats...

stages.raw: {"assumptions": [{"assumption": "More features will create the most value next", "status": "unverified", "if_wrong": "The project could become broader but less trusted"}], "framing_valid": true, "reframing": "The real question is how to compound trust without stalling product momentum.", "key_insight": "The repo competes on trust and inspectability, not feature maximalism alone."}

assumptions.assumption: More features will create the most value next
assumptions.if_wrong: The project could become broader but less trusted

parsed.reframing: The real question is how to compound trust without stalling product momentum.
parsed.key_insight: The repo competes on trust and inspectability, not feature maximalism alone.

stages.raw: {"symptom": "Pressure to add more scope quickly", "why_chain": [{"level": 1, "why": "Broader coverage looks like obvious progress", "evidence": "Security tools are often compared by rule counts and platform breadth"}, {"level": 2, "why": "External readers see visible features faster than internal quality work", "evidence": "Docs and packaging improvements are less headline-friendly"}, {"level": 3, "why": "Single-maintainer projects need visible momentum to attract users", "evidence": "Adoption often follows visible differentiation"}, {"level": 4, "why": "Feature momentum can substitute for trust-building when there is limited time", "evidence": "Maintenance capacity is finite"}, {"level": 5, "why": "The structural risk is confusing market-visible progress with compounding product quality", "evidence": "False positives and maintenance load rise faster than README bullets suggest"}], "root_cause": "The project risks optimizing for visible breadth over compounding trust discipline.", "structural_fix": "Tie expansion to benchmark fixtures, contributor ergonomics, and tighter release gates.", "key_insight": "The real bottleneck is maintainable quality control, not missing feature ideas."}

parsed.symptom: Pressure to add more scope quickly

why_chain.why: Broader coverage looks like obvious progress
why_chain.evidence: Security tools are often compared by rule counts and platform breadth

why_chain.why: External readers see visible features faster than internal quality work
why_chain.evidence: Docs and packaging improvements are less headline-friendly

why_chain.why: Single-maintainer projects need visible momentum to attract users
Feature momentum can substitute for trust-building when there is limited time
The structural risk is confusing market-visible progress with compounding product quality
why_chain.evidence: False positives and maintenance load rise faster than README bullets suggest

parsed.root_cause: The project risks optimizing for visible breadth over compounding trust discipline.
parsed.structural_fix: Tie expansion to benchmark fixtures, contributor ergonomics, and tighter release gates.
parsed.key_insight: The real bottleneck is maintainable quality control, not missing feature ideas.

stages.raw: {"hidden_dynamics": [{"actor": "maintainer", "protecting": "a high-trust reputation for local inspectable security tooling", "at_cost_of": "slower feature headline velocity"}], "misaligned_incentives": [{"between": ["user demand", "maintainable precision"], "conflict": "Users ask for more detections, but each rule family expands the calibration burden"}], "failure_modes": [{"mode": "The project becomes impressive to read but noisier to run", "likelihood": "medium", "impact": "high", "why_ignored": "Feature additions feel like obvious progress until signal quality degrades"}], "uncomfortable_truths": ["The most valuable next work may be less visible than new rules", "The README already creates expectations that future releases must now keep earning"], "key_insight": "The right strategic move is the one that protects signal quality, even if it looks slower from the outside."}

hidden_dynamics.protecting: a high-trust reputation for local inspectable security tooling

misaligned_incentives.conflict: Users ask for more detections, but each rule family expands the calibration burden

failure_modes.mode: The project becomes impressive to read but noisier to run

stages.raw: {"options": [{"name": "Expand Now", "approach": "Add more rules and platform coverage in the next cycle", "optimizes_for": "visible product breadth", "sacrifices": "calibration and contributor ergonomics", "success_probability": "medium", "risks": ["maintenance drag", "more suppression pressure"], "tradeoffs": "higher momentum, weaker trust compounding"}, {"name": "Trust First", "approach": "Strengthen benchmarks, examples, contributor workflow, and release discipline first", "optimizes_for": "durable adoption", "sacrifices": "short-term feature headlines", "success_probability": "high", "risks": ["slower surface expansion"], "tradeoffs": "less flashy, stronger long-term quality"}, {"name": "Staged Split", "approach": "Ship one carefully scoped rule family while simultaneously tightening trust infrastructure", "optimizes_for": "balance", "sacrifices": "speed on both fronts", "success_probability": "high", "risks": ["context switching"], "tradeoffs": "best strategic balance if scope stays disciplined"}], "comparison": "Trust First and Staged Split preserve the repo's strongest differentiator better than pure expansion.", "key_insight": "A staged split is safest if the maintainer needs visible progress without eroding trust."}

options.name: Expand Now
options.approach: Add more rules and platform coverage in the next cycle

options.name: Trust First
options.approach: Strengthen benchmarks, examples, contributor workflow, and release discipline first

options.name: Staged Split
options.approach: Ship one carefully scoped rule family while simultaneously tightening trust infrastructure
options.tradeoffs: best strategic balance if scope stays disciplined

parsed.comparison: Trust First and Staged Split preserve the repo's strongest differentiator better than pure expansion.
parsed.key_insight: A staged split is safest if the maintainer needs visible progress without eroding trust.

stages.raw: {"failure_modes": [{"what_failed": "Signal quality degraded as scope grew", "why": "New detections arrived faster than regression discipline", "warning_signs": ["rising false-positive complaints", "benchmark lag", "more undocumented edge cases"], "could_have_been_prevented_by": "locking feature growth to stronger examples, tests, and contributor boundaries"}], "most_likely_cause_of_death": "Growth without quality gates", "design_against": ["make fixtures mandatory for new rule families", "publish clearer contributor and release standards"], "key_insight": "The failure mode is strategic drift away from trust as the primary product."}

failure_modes.what_failed: Signal quality degraded as scope grew
failure_modes.why: New detections arrived faster than regression discipline
failure_modes.could_have_been_prevented_by: locking feature growth to stronger examples, tests, and contributor boundaries

design_against: make fixtures mandatory for new rule families
publish clearer contributor and release standards
parsed.key_insight: The failure mode is strategic drift away from trust as the primary product.

result.summary: The next release should prioritize trust-compounding work over pure scope expansion. If visible momentum is necessary, the safest path is a staged split: one narrow feature addition plus stronger examples, benchmarks, and contributor rails.
key_findings: The core product differentiator is trust and inspectability, not only breadth.
Unbounded scope growth would threaten the tool's strongest quality signal.
Contributor ergonomics and regression discipline are strategic work, not maintenance overhead.

risks_ranked.risk: Feature growth outpaces calibration and contributor support
risks_ranked.mitigation: Tie new scope to fixtures, benchmarks, and stricter contribution gates

result.recommended_action: Choose either Trust First or a tightly scoped staged split rather than feature-maximal expansion.
navigator_decisions_needed: What level of visible release velocity is necessary without weakening trust?
Which narrow rule family, if any, is worth shipping alongside trust hardening?
